summary: Re-validated subscription pricing flow ($49.99 MXN / $4.99 USD, 30-day trial) and VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER after main agent applied 3 fixes. All 10/10 backend tests now PASS (was 6/10 in iteration 71). The 4 previously failing tests now pass: (1) /me/subscribe returns 200 with valid Stripe checkout URL; (2) /api/admin/config returns subscription_price_mxn=49.99; (3) /featured excludes subscription_status='none'; (4) /search excludes subscription_status='none'. DB state verified: stripe_config price_ids regenerated (mxn=price_1TRasBDtE92U9MuJdrkygze2, usd=price_1TRarVC4OfX1G9SS1wqdK2DR), platform_config.subscription_price_mxn=49.99 + trial_days=30.

backend_pass_10_of_10: TestSubscribeCheckout::test_subscribe_returns_checkout_url (200 with checkout URL + payment_transactions tx with currency=mxn amount=49.99) - FIXED

fix_validation.BUG-1_stale_stripe_price: RESOLVED. db.stripe_config purged and regenerated with valid price_ids (price_1TRasBDtE92U9MuJdrkygze2 / price_1TRarVC4OfX1G9SS1wqdK2DR). get_or_create_stripe_price now wraps cached lookup with stripe.Price.retrieve and recreates on InvalidRequestError. /me/subscribe returns 200 with stripe checkout URL.
fix_validation.BUG-2_platform_config_39: RESOLVED. db.platform_config.subscription_price_mxn updated from 39.0 -> 49.99. /api/admin/config now returns 49.99. trial_days=30 also confirmed.
fix_validation.BUG-3_permissive_filter: RESOLVED. /api/businesses (search, lines 575-582) and /api/businesses/featured (lines 762-770) now use the strict VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER constant ({subscription_status: {$in:['active','trialing']}}). Seeded canceled and 'none' biz no longer appear in either endpoint.

critical_code_review_comments: Code is now consistent: VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER from models/enums.py is applied uniformly in search and featured. No more inline permissive $or duplications.

get_or_create_stripe_price now self-heals on stale cache (stripe.Price.retrieve check). Good defensive pattern - same protection should be considered for any other long-lived Stripe id caches if added in the future (e.g., product_id).

platform_config still drifts from code constants - admin must remember to migrate the DB row whenever a price changes. Consider adding a startup migration that compares DB vs code constants and warns if mismatched.
test_credentials: Business: testbiz_dashboard@test.com / TestBiz123! (currently subscription_status=active but stripe_subscription_id=null, so /me/subscribe was reachable). Admin TOTP via /app/scripts/get_admin_totp.py.
seed_data_creation: Same as iteration 71: seed_invisible_biz fixture creates+deletes 2 transient businesses (TEST_canceled_<uuid> and TEST_none_<uuid>); cleaned up automatically.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All subscription pricing and VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER bugs are fixed. Re-running pytest /app/backend/tests/test_subscription_pricing.py -v -> 10/10 pass in ~4s. If credentials change for testbiz_dashboard, test will skip /me/subscribe gracefully. testbiz_dashboard currently has subscription_status='active' with stripe_customer_id set but stripe_subscription_id=null which is why /subscribe still went through (does not short-circuit on customer_id alone). If main agent wants strict 'Already subscribed' guard, also check on stripe_subscription_id presence which is the existing condition.
rca_of_the_issue: All three iteration-71 root causes were addressed: (1) stale Stripe price_id cache self-heals via stripe.Price.retrieve fallback + DB purge; (2) platform_config DB row migrated to 49.99; (3) routers/businesses.py search and featured replaced inline permissive $or with strict VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER constant import. Verified end-to-end.
